verdict_reasoning: SRC is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or intrinsic membrane-anchoring mechanism. It is soluble and localized primarily to the cytoplasm, where it functions as an intracellular signaling molecule downstream of receptor tyrosine kinases. While SRC participates in signaling cascades initiated by surface receptors, the protein body itself does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. No cell-state-induced ecto-pool, tissue-restricted surface display, lysosomal exocytosis, dual PM-localization with stable anchoring, or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering has been documented for SRC. The protein is not the target of cell-surface-directed therapeutics.
